headline: GENYOUTH AND TASTE OF THE NFL EXPAND ACCESS TO 75 MILLION SCHOOL MEALS FOR STUDENTS NATIONWIDE
summary: GENYOUth, the national nonprofit organization dedicated to helping schoolchildren thrive by living well-nourished and physically active lives, today announced that its 2026 Taste of the NFL event and accompanying initiatives will deliver a combined impact of $1.5 million. The funding will reach 500 schools benefiting 275,000students by increasing access to 75million school meals and strengthening nutrition security for young people across all 32 NFL club markets.

headline: Tesla Begins Mass Producing Semi Trucks. Stock Dips.
summary: Tesla's dedicated high-volume production line at its Sparks, Nevada facility successfully manufactured its first Semi trucks.  Wednesday's announcement, made via an X post, caps a nearly decade-long wait for mass production of Tesla's commercial trucks, which were first shown the public in 2017.  The Tesla Semi truck comes in two versions: the Standard Range with 325 miles and the Long Range with 500 miles.

headline: Food giants hit with consumer lawsuit calling ultraprocessed ingredients addictive
summary: The complaint against Kraft Heinz, PepsiCo and other companies aims to connect packaged foods and consumer health issues after a judge tossed a similar lawsuit last fall.

headline: PepsiCo Bottling Shift In Northern Europe And What It Means For Valuation
summary: PepsiCo plans to end its bottling and distribution partnership with Royal Unibrew in Denmark, Finland, and the Baltic states in 2028. The change will affect how PepsiCo products are produced and distributed across key Northern European markets. The company will need to put new arrangements in place for local production, supply chains, and customer relationships once the agreement concludes.
